response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine tooling and IDE-adjacent experience (LabWindows/CVI components at National Instruments, test runners and developer tooling in D), but his core stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang rather than the JVM. Java appears only briefly and there is no evidence of Kotlin, Spring, or enterprise JVM framework experience — the central required qualifications for this role. Strong craftsmanship signals cannot offset the missing JVM depth for a role focused on Java/Kotlin/Spring IDE subsystems.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Built developer tooling and test infrastructure (Trial test runner, Fluent-Asserts, MCP code generators)
Long track record across complex systems, including high-throughput low-latency services
concerns: No demonstrated Kotlin or Spring/enterprise JVM framework experience
Java exposure is minimal and dated relative to the role's core requirement
Career focus has been web/Node.js/DLang, not JVM language tooling or enterprise build systems
